author: orionriver@gmail.com
text:
FRAMING UPDATE — bl-2a77 (delivery/worktree plugin) closed concurrently (2026-06-02) and already resolved the FORGE slice of scope item #3: the forge-gated delivery variant supplies its OWN `close.pre` gate-enforcement plugin, because greenfield core no longer has the gates-blocks-close check (bl-dd3e removed all close enforcement). 'Sequence enforcement is a plugin pre concern, not core.' So the forge gate-child handoff is SETTLED — treat it as a worked example / settled input, not open.

REMAINING open scope (what bl-2a77 did NOT cover):
- The GENERAL `gates` link — a user-facing typed link between ARBITRARY tasks (e.g. we just added bl-dd3e gates bl-2a77 by hand, nothing to do with forge). Its core 'blocks close while target open' enforcement still needs removing/re-homing. The forge gate-child is ONE consumer; the general link is broader.
- `depends_on` (blocks CLAIM of the dependent until the dep closes) — bl-2a77 didn't touch this at all. Still core enforcement on a different transition (claim, not close).
- Do the link TYPES stay as pure core metadata (yes, leaning) with only the ENFORCEMENT moving out? And to where for the GENERAL case — skill hinting, or a bundled default `close.pre`/`claim.pre` plugin that ships with core but is severable?
- Does bl-2e26 gain a § for the link model (it has none today)?

So this topic narrows to: the generic enforcing-link semantics, with the forge case as a settled precedent (bl-2a77) to stay consistent with. (Note: could not add a relates_to link to bl-2a77 — link add refuses a closed target; relation captured here in prose.)

author: mark
text: RESOLVED as subsumed (topic bl-a408, now consolidated into bl-2e26 §3 + §10). The enforcing pair (gates/depends_on) is unified as the blocker primitive `blockers:[{id,on}]` on the blocked task: depends_on = a claim-blocker, gates = a close-blocker — one edge parameterized by `on`. Enforcement re-homed to the default GATING PLUGIN (claim.pre/close.pre reject); core stores the schema and enforces nothing. The one residual this topic raised that bl-a408 didn't state explicitly: the PURE-METADATA links (relates_to, supersedes, duplicates, replies_to) STAY as core metadata with no enforcement — now recorded in bl-2e26 §3. bl-2e26 gained the §10 link/blocker model it lacked. Nothing left open; closing.
